chunk_summary: This section focuses on the criteria and dosing guidelines for initiating iron supplementation during the rehabilitation phase of severe acute malnutrition (SAM) management, emphasizing infection control and metabolic corrections.
learning_objectives: Understand the criteria for initiating iron supplementation in children with SAM during rehabilitation.
Learn the recommended dosing guidelines for iron supplementation in SAM management.
Recognize the importance of infection management and metabolic corrections in SAM rehabilitation.
Identify key references and guidelines for evidence-based SAM care.
key_facts: Iron supplementation should only be initiated during the rehabilitation phase of SAM, not during the stabilization phase.
The recommended daily dose of elemental iron is 3 mg/kg body weight per day, as per WHO guidelines.
Iron supplementation is contraindicated in the presence of ongoing infections or severe anemia until stabilized.
Infection management and metabolic corrections must precede iron initiation to avoid complications.
The Indian Academy of Pediatrics and WHO provide standardized protocols for SAM management, including iron dosing.
reference_quote: Iron supplementation should be initiated during the rehabilitation phase at a dose of 3 mg/kg/day of elemental iron, as per WHO guidelines. (Source: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK154454/)
